requirement_title: No rate limiting on login endpoint
title: EP - Brute-force login attempts not throttled (valid class)
test_steps:
Prepare a list of 100 invalid login credentials (e.g., random usernames and passwords)
Send the 100 login requests as rapidly as possible (within 1 second)
test_data: requests: 100, frequency: ~100/sec, credentials: invalid (e.g., username='nonexistent', password='wrong')
expected_result: All 100 requests receive HTTP 401 Unauthorized responses. No HTTP 429 Too Many Requests or any other rate limiting responses are observed.

requirement_title: No rate limiting on login endpoint
title: DT - Brute-force condition triggers no throttling
test_steps: Send a burst of 500 invalid login attempts in quick succession (e.g., 500 requests with zero delay between them)
test_data: requests: 500, interval: 0 ms, credentials: random strings (e.g., username='attacker', password='guess')
expected_result: System does not throttle; all 500 attempts receive authentication error responses (HTTP 401) without any HTTP 429 rate limiting responses.

requirement_title: No rate limiting on login endpoint
title: State transition path 1 (ALL_TRANSITIONS)
expected_result: The login attempt is processed without throttling; authentication proceeds normally.
